PROP_8e0b6316.rationale: Annual pay reviews are the overwhelming UK norm; CIPD/XpertHR reward surveys consistently show ~80-90% of organisations on an annual cycle.
PROP_8e0b6316.drivers: FTE_Band/F: small informal orgs shift toward ad hoc; large toward Annually

PROP_dff9a2a5.rationale: In a normal review year most UK employers award increases to most staff; across-the-board or near-universal awards are the norm, especially where pay spines apply.
PROP_dff9a2a5.drivers: Budget_Flexibility: Tight shifts down a band; Public Sector/unionised toward 95-100% (spine points)

PROP_e1d1e604.rationale: Off-cycle increases are exception-based for most employers (retention/counter-offer), typically affecting a small minority of staff.
PROP_e1d1e604.drivers: Turnover_Band/Talent_Competition: high-churn, hot-market orgs make more off-cycle awards

PROP_36b990f9.rationale: Auto-enrolment floor is 3% employer; private-sector typical employer contributions cluster around 4-8% (ONS pension participation data), public sector materially higher.
PROP_36b990f9.drivers: Ownership=Public Sector -> 11%+ (DB schemes); FTE_Band up -> higher band; derived from same latent value as the by-level pension matrices

SICK_01_88ce3d5c.rationale: A clear majority of UK employers pay some occupational sick pay above SSP; minority statutory-only, concentrated in smaller and high-frontline employers.
SICK_01_88ce3d5c.drivers: F/FTE_Band up -> Yes; high-frontline hospitality/retail down

REW_BEN_SICK_001.rationale: Mirror of SICK_01 with the enhanced majority split between enhanced-only and enhanced+SSP combination.
REW_BEN_SICK_001.drivers: Consistent with SICK_01; statutory-only orgs answer 'Statutory sick pay only'

REW_BEN_SICK_002.rationale: Common UK schemes run weeks-to-months, with public-sector style 6-month full/half pay at the generous end.
REW_BEN_SICK_002.drivers: Tied to OSP offer; larger/public orgs longer durations (26-week full/half-pay schemes)

REW_BEN_SICK_004.rationale: Where OSP exists most schemes pay from day one or after SSP-style waiting days; statutory-only employers map to Not applicable.
REW_BEN_SICK_004.drivers: Tied to OSP offer (statutory-only -> Not applicable); waiting days commoner in frontline-heavy orgs

CONT_01_7e020202.rationale: Three and six months are the two standard UK probation lengths, between them covering the large majority of employers.
CONT_01_7e020202.drivers: FTE_Band up -> 6 months commoner; small orgs more 3-month/varies

REW_FAI_079.rationale: Gender pay gap reporting is mandatory for 250+ employee employers (gov.uk), so analysis is near-universal there and a voluntary minority practice below the threshold.
REW_FAI_079.drivers: FTE_Band: 250+ -> Yes ~0.93 (statutory reporting duty); <250 voluntary minority

REW_BEN_HOL_001.rationale: 25 days plus bank holidays is the modal UK private-sector entitlement; public sector commonly 27-30+.
REW_BEN_HOL_001.drivers: Ownership=Public/Charity & FTE_Band up -> higher bands; small founder-led -> lower

REW_BEN_HOL_003.rationale: Holiday purchase schemes are a sizeable-minority practice (roughly a third of employers, skewed large); selling is much rarer.
REW_BEN_HOL_003.drivers: FTE_Band/flex-benefits maturity up -> buy schemes commoner

REW_BEN_HOL_004.rationale: Length-of-service holiday accrual is mainstream, roughly half of employers.

REW_BEN_HOL_005.rationale: Most employers allow some carry-over, typically capped (commonly 5 days); pure use-it-or-lose-it is the minority.
REW_BEN_HOL_005.drivers: None strong — baseline with light F modulation

REW_BEN_HOL_006.rationale: Where purchase schemes exist the cap is most commonly one working week.
REW_BEN_HOL_006.drivers: Conditional on REW_BEN_HOL_003: no buy scheme -> Not applicable; else typically one week

REW_BEN_HOL_007.rationale: Mandatory shutdowns are a minority practice concentrated in manufacturing/construction (seasonal closures).

REW_INC_103.rationale: Most private-sector employers operate some bonus and increasingly broad-based eligibility; bonuses remain rare in public sector and charities.
REW_INC_103.drivers: Ownership: PLC/PE -> broad eligibility; Public Sector/Charity -> None dominates; drives the bonus matrices

WEL_MEN_021.rationale: EAPs are one of the most prevalent UK benefits, around three-quarters or more of employers and near-universal among large ones.
WEL_MEN_021.drivers: FTE_Band/F up -> Yes (EAPs near-universal in large employers)

WEL_MH_02.drivers: Follows EAP prevalence; F up -> most employees

REW_BEN_045.rationale: Group life cover reaches roughly half of employers (skewed to mid/large), with 2-4x salary the standard multiples.
REW_BEN_045.drivers: FTE_Band/Budget_Flexibility up -> offered and higher multiples

REW_PAY_001.rationale: Formal pay structures are majority practice among established/larger employers and uncommon in small founder-led firms; the blend across the seed mix sits near half.
REW_PAY_001.drivers: F (HR_Maturity/FTE_Band/Ownership): formal ranges scale strongly with size and governance

REW_PAY_002.rationale: Job-evaluated grading is standard in large/public organisations, rare in small ones.

REW_PAY_003.rationale: Annual market refresh is the standard cadence where structures exist; ad hoc dominates otherwise.
REW_PAY_003.drivers: F up -> annual refresh; low-F -> ad hoc/not reviewed

REW_PAY_006.rationale: Most employers use some external pay data; systematic multi-source use is confined to the sophisticated end.
REW_PAY_006.drivers: F: data-led pay management scales with maturity and size

REW_FAI_087.rationale: Documented pay transparency remains minority practice in the UK, with a sizeable in-development cohort ahead of EU-style requirements.
REW_FAI_087.drivers: F/size up; EU directive horizon pushes 'In development'

REW_FAI_088.rationale: Sharing ranges with employees lags having ranges; minority-to-mixed practice.

REW_FAI_089.rationale: Salary-on-advert is standard in the public sector and a growing minority elsewhere.
REW_FAI_089.drivers: Public Sector -> All roles (standard practice); private minority

PRO_COLLBARG_0a071dd7.rationale: ONS trade-union statistics put collective-bargaining coverage near a quarter of employees, heavily concentrated in the public sector and unionised industries.
PRO_COLLBARG_0a071dd7.drivers: Workforce_Unionised_%: >40% -> pay+core dominant; ~0% -> No (ONS: ~25% coverage economy-wide, concentrated in public sector)

MET_1fa3fa15.rationale: ONS sickness-absence rate runs ~2% economy-wide with higher rates in public sector, health and frontline-heavy work.
MET_1fa3fa15.drivers: Workforce_Frontline_% and Public Sector -> higher bands; office-based -> lower

MET_7f79a965.rationale: UK voluntary turnover typically averages low-to-mid teens; the registry already assigns each org a turnover band, which the answer must echo.
MET_7f79a965.drivers: Turnover_Band (registry) anchors the reported band directly

ATTR_OE_04.rationale: First-year leaver rates typically run somewhat above all-staff turnover; anchored to each org's turnover band.

ONB_03.rationale: Very-early attrition is a small single-digit share for most employers, higher in high-churn frontline sectors.

ATTR_HE_07.rationale: The overwhelming majority of UK new hires pass probation; failure/extension is exceptional.
ATTR_HE_07.drivers: Light F effect (tracking); high churn shifts down slightly

EXT_REW_GAP_009.rationale: ONS/industry surveys show hybrid (2-3 office days) as the dominant pattern for remote-capable UK roles; fully-remote is a small minority.
EXT_REW_GAP_009.drivers: Light only — hybrid norms are broadly cross-sector for desk-based roles

WEL_FLE_030.rationale: Statutory right-to-request plus mainstream practice makes some flexible-working access near-universal; formality scales with size.
WEL_FLE_030.drivers: F/size -> formal policy (day-one statutory right to request since 2024)

WEL_FLE_031.rationale: Frontline/shift operations constrain flexibility to role subsets in most organisations.

REW_PAY_011.rationale: Paid overtime is standard for hourly/shift populations and uncommon for salaried professional ones.
REW_PAY_011.drivers: Workforce_Shift_%/Frontline_% high -> Yes ~0.9; low-frontline professional firms -> No

OT_03_1a68c68a.rationale: TOIL is widespread, particularly in public/third sector where paid overtime is restricted.

REW_BEN_FAM_001.rationale: Roughly half of UK employers enhance maternity pay, strongly size-graded.

REW_BEN_FAM_002.rationale: Where enhanced, 6-26 weeks of enhanced pay is the common range.

REW_BEN_FAM_006.rationale: A growing minority offer paid parental leave beyond statutory.

REW_BEN_102.rationale: Pension salary sacrifice is standard among large employers and rarer among small ones; participation where offered is broad.
REW_BEN_102.drivers: FTE_Band up -> offered and higher participation (pension salary sacrifice standard in large employers)

REW_PAY_TRONC_6db81475.rationale: Troncs exist where tips exist — hospitality and parts of leisure; meaningless elsewhere.
REW_PAY_TRONC_6db81475.drivers: Sector: Hospitality -> Yes dominant (tipping economy); others Not applicable

PRO_GOV_TRONC_IND_3cc52941.rationale: Conditional detail of the tronc question; independent troncmasters are the compliant norm.
PRO_GOV_TRONC_IND_3cc52941.drivers: Conditional on REW_PAY_TRONC (no tronc -> Not applicable)

REW_FAI_TRONC_TYPES_b5547658.rationale: Card tips/service charge dominate modern tronc flows.

PROP_8c304d7c.rationale: Industry-cited EAP utilisation typically runs mid-single-digits to ~12%.

REW_BEN_044.rationale: PMI reaches roughly half of employers (size-graded) and is classically grade-restricted or post-probation.
REW_BEN_044.drivers: Conditional on PMI in REW_BEN_038; grade-restriction the classic pattern; drives PMI matrices

ALLOW_01.rationale: Allowance prevalence follows workforce shape: shift/on-call pay where shift work exists; car allowances for managerial/field populations; location weighting in London.
ALLOW_01.drivers: Workforce_Shift_% -> shift/on-call; HQ_Region London -> location allowance; richness -> car/mobile

REW_PAY_016.rationale: As ALLOW_01, with night/weekend/bank-holiday premiums tied to shift operations.
REW_PAY_016.drivers: As ALLOW_01 — premiums driven by Workforce_Shift_%, kept consistent with ALLOW_01 and the allowance-value matrix

REW_BEN_038.rationale: Core-benefit prevalence ordering (EAP and life assurance high; PMI ~half, size-graded; cash plans/dental mid; niche items low) reflects published UK benefit surveys.
REW_BEN_038.drivers: FTE_Band + Budget_Flexibility (richness R) scale the odds of every benefit; PMI selection drives PMI-dependent questions

EXT_REW_GAP_005.rationale: Where long-service awards exist, 10/20/25-year milestones are the classic pattern.
EXT_REW_GAP_005.drivers: Conditional on the long-service-scheme question; decade milestones dominate

EXT_REW_GAP_011.rationale: Employers routinely provide equipment for home workers; ongoing broadband/utility funding is rare.
EXT_REW_GAP_011.drivers: Low Workforce_Frontline_% (desk-based) -> equipment provision standard; high-frontline -> None

PRO_UNIONSCOPE_53b16ffb.rationale: Recognition follows union density: frontline/operational groups are organised first; whole-workforce agreements only at high density (public-sector pattern).
PRO_UNIONSCOPE_53b16ffb.drivers: Workforce_Unionised_% determines recognition and which groups (frontline/ops first; 'all employees' at very high coverage)

HR_OP_08.rationale: Payroll is the most commonly outsourced HR service in the UK (roughly half of employers); ER almost never is.
HR_OP_08.drivers: Small orgs outsource payroll more; very large bring it in-house or to shared services

REW_INC_060.rationale: Mainstream bonus design blends company financials with individual performance; sales/customer measures sector-dependent.
REW_INC_060.drivers: Conditional on bonus existing (REW_INC_103); company financial + individual blend is the dominant design

REW_PAY_023.rationale: Affordability and inflation lead every published pay-planning survey; NLW pressure is specific to low-pay/frontline employers.
REW_PAY_023.drivers: High Workforce_Frontline_% -> NLW pressure; Workforce_Unionised_% -> union factor

PROP_c177397f.rationale: Right-to-work checks are a legal requirement; references near-universal; DBS and credit checks are sector-specific.
PROP_c177397f.drivers: Right-to-work statutory for all; criminal checks -> Healthcare/Education/Charity/Public; credit checks -> Financial Services

WEL_BMAP_FIN_SALARY_SACRIFICE_001.rationale: Pension salary sacrifice and Cycle-to-Work are the mainstream schemes; childcare vouchers closed to new entrants in 2018.
WEL_BMAP_FIN_SALARY_SACRIFICE_001.drivers: FTE_Band up -> pension salary sacrifice; childcare vouchers low (scheme closed to new entrants 2018)

matrix_drivers.323ffcf1-749b-43f3-bf34-1de6b8b1ca67: Max bonus % by level — laddered Board>…>Frontline; scale by Ownership (PLC/PE high, Public/Charity none); conditional on REW_INC_103.
matrix_drivers.REW_INC_111: Target bonus % by level — ~55% of max bonus ladder, same conditions.
matrix_drivers.REW_INC_133: LTI eligibility by level — PLC/PE top levels only; None for Public/Charity/most founder-led.
matrix_drivers.REW_INC_LTI_MAX_01: Max LTI % by level — conditional on eligibility; Board-heavy ladder.
matrix_drivers.REW_BEN_112: Typical employer pension % by level — from org latent pension value (Public Sector high), small exec uplift; consistent with PROP_36b990f9 band.
matrix_drivers.3faf1f0c-f753-497f-a395-384bba38c5e3: PMI premium — Single<Partner<Family realistic premium ladder; conditional on PMI offered.
matrix_drivers.REW_BEN_139: PMI eligibility by level — top-down threshold from REW_BEN_044 breadth.
matrix_drivers.fa0f46f6-61e3-41d1-a2d1-3e57483bb1cf: Car allowance £ by level — laddered Board~£8–14k -> Manager~£3–5k; conditional on car allowance offered.
matrix_drivers.REW_PAY_109: Status car eligibility — top-down threshold, conditional on car allowance.
matrix_drivers.ATT_HIR_AGENCY_RATE_01: Agency % by level — rises toward frontline; scaled by Workforce_Shift_/Frontline_%.
matrix_drivers.ATT_HIR_CPH_01: Cost-per-hire % of pay — falls toward frontline (senior search premium).
matrix_drivers.ATT_HIR_REPLACE_COST_01: Replacement cost % salary — laddered, senior high.
matrix_drivers.ATT_OBO_REGRET_ATTR_01: Regretted attrition % — scaled from registry Turnover_Band, lower at top levels.
matrix_drivers.MET_cd8efe96: Time to hire days — Board~90 -> Frontline~3 weeks ladder.
matrix_drivers.GRO_CAR_042: Internal fill % — mid-level bump, lower at entry and Board.
matrix_drivers.GRO_LEA_LD_SPEND_01: L&D spend % of pay — 0.5–3% range, F-scaled, slight senior uplift.
matrix_drivers.GRO_POT_041: Succession coverage — depth scales with F (Advanced cover to Manager; Basic Board-only or none).
matrix_drivers.INC_Q308527: Representation % — sector-realistic gender mix; ethnicity London-weighted; other rows population-plausible.
matrix_drivers.b1785613-96ed-4a64-9fd7-762d0ac65f19: Employee notice — 1–4wk frontline to 12wk+ Board ladder (categorical weeks).
matrix_drivers.REW_Q524161: Employer notice — mirrors employee notice, never below statutory.
matrix_drivers.REW_Q528801: Overtime multiplier by shift — 1x day to 1.5–2x bank holiday; shift orgs only.
matrix_drivers.REW_Q534581: Pay multipliers by time — 1.0x standard rising to 1.5–2x bank holiday.
matrix_drivers.PROP_168eb098: Span of control — banded; widest at supervisor/frontline, narrow at Board; frontline-heavy orgs wider.
matrix_drivers.REW_PAY_020: Allowance pensionability — mostly No (allowances typically non-pensionable), Yes commoner in public sector.
matrix_drivers.a7ed418e-b057-4b70-ab58-31e897b7c1b6: Allowance £ values — only for allowances the org offers (REW_PAY_016); realistic £ ranges per allowance type.
matrix_drivers.REW_FAI_TRONC_GROUPS_e5639ac2: Tronc participation by level — hospitality only, frontline/supervisor groups.

numeric_drivers.PROP_d4b98d24: Operating profit per FTE — margin on revenue/FTE; low for charity/public (estimate).
numeric_drivers.PROP_acdc8dd9: HR tech spend £40–350 per employee, systems-maturity-tilted.
numeric_drivers.PROP_9e3b1d18: Early attrition % — registry Turnover_Band × ~1.3 with noise.
